tasks_completed.description: Add --repo-root optional argument to execute-push.py argparse block
tasks_completed.changes: Added parser.add_argument('--repo-root', required=False, default=None, ...) immediately before the --auto argument definition
tasks_completed.rationale: AC1 requirement: --repo-root must be optional with default=None for backward compat

tasks_completed.description: Insert os.chdir guard in main() after _parse_args() returns
tasks_completed.changes: Added Step 1b block: if args.repo_root is not None: try os.chdir(args.repo_root) except OSError: print stderr, return 2. Placed between _parse_args() return and repo_hash = args.repo_hash (before session_id check and all git operations).
tasks_completed.rationale: AC2/AC3: chdir must fire before session_id check and grant file access; OSError guard exits 2 before touching any grant file

tasks_completed.description: Update module docstring Usage block and exit codes
tasks_completed.changes: Added '[--repo-root <REPO_ROOT>] \' line to Usage block between --request-id and [--auto]. Updated exit code 2 description to include 'or invalid --repo-root path (OSError on chdir)'.
tasks_completed.rationale: AC6: docstring must show --repo-root in usage and exit codes. Note: docstring says 'invalid --repo-root path (OSError on chdir)' not 'non-git' because non-git dirs fall through to the existing CalledProcessError handler (exit 1) — that is a pre-existing guard (removal_authorized=false). The chdir exit 2 only fires on OSError (path not found/not a dir).

tasks_completed.description: Update push.md Step 2 to capture REPO_ROOT variable
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced single-line 'REPO_HASH=$(printf ... realpath ... git rev-parse --show-toplevel ...' with two lines: 'REPO_ROOT=$(realpath "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)")' then 'REPO_HASH=$(printf '%s' "${REPO_ROOT}" | sha256sum | cut -c1-16)'
tasks_completed.rationale: AC4: canonical two-line form required. REPO_ROOT captured with realpath at snapshot time; REPO_HASH derives from ${REPO_ROOT} directly (no second git call, no nested realpath)

tasks_completed.description: Update push.md Step 5 invocation blocks: remove venv activation, add --repo-root
tasks_completed.changes: Removed 'source venv/bin/activate &&' prefix from both invocation blocks. Added '--repo-root "${REPO_ROOT}" \' continuation line in each block. In the auto block, --repo-root appears immediately before --auto.
tasks_completed.rationale: AC5: python3 used directly (no venv needed — stdlib only). --repo-root passes the snapshot value to execute-push.py regardless of orchestrator CWD at Step 5 time.

git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: execute-push.py was written assuming it would always be invoked from the correct repo root. No mechanism bound the script to a specific git repo; process CWD was the sole resolver.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: os.chdir(args.repo_root) at script startup (before any git operation or grant access) binds the entire process — including push.sh inherited via os.execv — to the repo root captured at Step 2 time.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: 28 lines added across 2 files. execute-push.py requires: 6 lines for argparse entry, 9 lines for chdir block + blank lines, 2 lines for docstring updates = 17 lines minimum. push.md requires: 1 line added to Step 2 (REPO_ROOT capture), 2 lines modified in Step 5 (venv removal + --repo-root in each block) = ~11 net. Total diff is minimally scoped to the 5 specified acceptance criteria; no extraneous changes.

dev.qa_notes: AC7 behavioral test: run 'cd /tmp && python3 /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py --repo-root /root --repo-hash aaaa --branch test --remote origin --request-id req1' with no CLAUDE_SESSION_ID in env. Expected: exit 2 with 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is not set or empty' (not a git error). Confirmed passing. Static AC checks: AC1/AC3 (argparse required=False, default=None), AC2 (chdir block precedes session_id check at line ~110), AC4 (push.md Step 2 two-line form), AC5 (no venv prefix, --repo-root before --auto in both blocks), AC6 (docstring updated).

self_verification.notes: python3 -m py_compile scripts/execute-push.py: syntax OK. AC7 behavioral test: cd /tmp && python3 .../execute-push.py --repo-root /root --repo-hash aaaa --branch test --remote origin --request-id req1 => exit 2, stderr: 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is not set or empty. Invoke /push from within a Claude Code session.' Confirms chdir to /root succeeded before session_id check.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 4 findings: (1) BLOCKER — docstring says 'invalid/non-git --repo-root => exit 2' but non-git dirs exit 1 from existing CalledProcessError handler; (2) BLOCKER — --repo-root not bound to --repo-hash (defense-in-depth); (3) MAJOR — smoke test doesn't cover full regression path with two repos; (4) OBSERVATION_ONLY — scope note about Step 2/push-analyst CWD.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 (in_scope_minor): Fixed docstring to say 'invalid --repo-root path (OSError on chdir)' rather than 'invalid/non-git' — accurate because the chdir OSError only fires on path errors, not git-repo absence. Finding 2 (out_of_scope): BA spec explicitly excludes repo-hash binding validation from scope; defense-in-depth not requested. Finding 3 (out_of_scope): test_writer_expected=false per context JSON; AC7 as specified in BA spec is the required behavioral test. Finding 4 (OBSERVATION_ONLY): confirmed by BA scope.

recommendations: QA should verify AC7 behavioral test: cd /tmp && python3 scripts/execute-push.py --repo-root /root --repo-hash aaaa --branch test --remote origin --request-id req1 (no CLAUDE_SESSION_ID) => exit 2 with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID message
Codex finding 2 (--repo-root not validated against --repo-hash) is out of scope for this cycle but worth tracking as a defense-in-depth improvement for a future cycle
